thread.title: Russia ramps up the rhetoric, challenging West in continuing support for Ukraine
thread.title_full: Russia ramps up the rhetoric, challenging West in continuing support for Ukraine

author: AUSTIN DENEAN | The National News Desk
title: Russia ramps up the rhetoric, challenging West in continuing support for Ukraine
text: {p}Russia is ramping up its threats to Western nations that have backed Ukraine’s defense of its invasion with the launch of a nuclear-capable missile and {a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/top-videos/putin-signs-russia-revised-nuclear-doctrine-after-us-president-joe-biden-support-for-ukraines-missile-strikes-western-supplied-longer-range-missiles-nato-zelenskyy-kyiv#\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/top-videos/putin-signs-russia-revised-nuclear-doctrine-after-us-president-joe-biden-support-for-ukraines-missile-strikes-western-supplied-longer-range-missiles-nato-zelenskyy-kyiv#\"}changes to its nuclear doctrine {/a}in response to the approval of American and British weapons being approved to strike deeper into Russia.{/p}{p}The launch of an experimental missile, named Oreshnik, into Ukraine on Thursday marked another escalation in the conflict that has dragged on for nearly three years. Russian President Vladimir Putin said the missile flies at ten times the speed of sound and could be turned on any nation whose missiles are used to attack Russia.{/p}{p}The test of the missile comes just days after President Joe Biden reversed a long-standing decision to allow Ukraine to use U.S.-provided missiles to strike inside Russian territory. The delivery of the Army Tactical Missile System, ballistic missiles with a range of 190 miles, is not thought to be sufficient to change the outcome of the war but it seems to have sparked new concerns inside the Kremlin that more powerful missiles in the future.{/p}{p}“This is a ground war that's a stalemate, and it's a war of attrition, and now it's the battle of wills,” said Javed Ali, a former counterterrorism official in federal government and associate professor of practice at the University of Michigan. “Even with that capability that Ukraine has now, it can't hit targets deeper in Russia, and the further they try to move them to the border, the more they also become a target. The Russians could hit those things on their side, just from across the border as well. This is the game of cat-and-mouse that's going to go on. But I don't think they qualitatively change the nature of the fighting.”{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-embed-file=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/CkKZqB1RA88?si=odPFLN9CxyLhO4yo\" data-embed-type=\"youtube\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}After the first missile was launched into Russia, the Kremlin has ramped up its rhetoric and escalated its moves in a war that has mostly been a stalemate with little gains in either direction. Putin and other top Kremlin officials have issued direct warnings to Western allies of Ukraine about providing military aid to defend its invasion, including threats that its new missile {a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/nato-and-ukraine-to-hold-emergency-talks-after-russian-hypersonic-missile-strike-putin-kremlin-oreshnik-missile-retaliation-kyiv-western-longer-range-missiles-north-korean-troops-kim-jong-un-zelenskyy#\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/nato-and-ukraine-to-hold-emergency-talks-after-russian-hypersonic-missile-strike-putin-kremlin-oreshnik-missile-retaliation-kyiv-western-longer-range-missiles-north-korean-troops-kim-jong-un-zelenskyy#\"}could be directed at NATO-allied countries.{/a}{/p}{p}“We believe that we have the right to use our weapons against military facilities of the countries that allow to use their weapons against our facilities,” Putin said in a televised speech to Russians.{/p}{p}President Joe Biden had resisted Ukrainian entreaties to approve the use of long-range missiles striking inside Ukraine for months over concerns about Russian escalation. The administration reversed that decision after North Korean troops were brought to help Russia in a new escalation of the war.{/p}{p}An initial hesitation to ramp up the type of military aid or capabilities to Ukraine before ultimately relenting has been a somewhat consistent theme throughout the war from the U.S. The White House has reversed initial rejections of requests for advanced tanks and fighter jets only to reverse course months later.{/p}{p}“It should not cause any of the friends of Ukraine to change course, that's the important thing,” said Ian Kelly, ambassador in residence at Northwestern University and a former State Department diplomat. “He's had some success in getting the Biden administration and some central European countries to kind of self-deter, and not give the Ukrainians really what they need in terms of air support and then artillery support for fear of triggering Putin.”{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-alt=\"AP24326521776338.jpg\" data-caption=\"In%20this%20photo%20provided%20by%20the%20Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Services%20on%20Nov.%2021,%202024,%20rescue%20workers%20put%20out%20a%20fire%20of%20a%20building%20which%20was%20heavily%20damaged%20by%20a%20Russian%20strike%20on%20Dnipro,%20Ukraine.%20(Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Service%20via%20AP)\" data-duration=\"\" data-externalid=\"7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e\" data-mediatype=\"image\" data-renditions=\"%7B%22large16x9%22:%7B%22blurSigma%22:-1,%22height%22:576,%22label%22:%22large16x9%22,%22scaleWidth%22:986,%22url%22:%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/986/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22,%22width%22:1024,%22x%22:0,%22y%22:53,%22uuid%22:null,%22id%22:%22196123a5-1876-404b-8acb-ac728ad5c2a7%22,%22preciseWidth%22:null,%22preciseHeight%22:null,%22name%22:%22large16x9Url%22,%22rendition%22:%22large16x9%22,%22labelOrConvertedName%22:%22large16x9%22%7D,%22jumbo16x9%22:%7B%22blurSigma%22:-1,%22height%22:576,%22label%22:%22jumbo16x9%22,%22scaleWidth%22:1320,%22url%22:%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/1320/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22,%22width%22:1024,%22x%22:0,%22y%22:53,%22uuid%22:null,%22id%22:%2294cb5127-5cb3-49dd-886f-c4fce1bda84a%22,%22preciseWidth%22:null,%22preciseHeight%22:null,%22name%22:%22jumbo16x9Url%22,%22rendition%22:%22jumbo16x9%22,%22labelOrConvertedName%22:%22jumbo16x9%22%7D,%22small16x9%22:%7B%22blurSigma%22:-1,%22height%22:576,%22label%22:%22small16x9%22,%22scaleWidth%22:160,%22url%22:%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/160/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22,%22width%22:1024,%22x%22:0,%22y%22:53,%22uuid%22:null,%22id%22:%222759f400-8a05-4d71-8805-8857974a2864%22,%22preciseWidth%22:null,%22preciseHeight%22:null,%22name%22:%22small16x9Url%22,%22rendition%22:%22small16x9%22,%22labelOrConvertedName%22:%22small16x9%22%7D,%22full16x9%22:%7B%22blurSigma%22:-1,%22height%22:576,%22label%22:%22full16x9%22,%22scaleWidth%22:1024,%22url%22:%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/1024/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22,%22width%22:1024,%22x%22:0,%22y%22:53,%22uuid%22:null,%22id%22:%22a01bf78a-bb13-494c-9083-9adebfaea255%22,%22preciseWidth%22:null,%22preciseHeight%22:null,%22name%22:%22full16x9Url%22,%22rendition%22:%22full16x9%22,%22labelOrConvertedName%22:%22full16x9%22%7D,%22medium16x9%22:%7B%22blurSigma%22:-1,%22height%22:576,%22label%22:%22medium16x9%22,%22scaleWidth%22:648,%22url%22:%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/648/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22,%22width%22:1024,%22x%22:0,%22y%22:53,%22uuid%22:null,%22id%22:%22686158e3-479a-461f-b42f-432b661e8334%22,%22preciseWidth%22:null,%22preciseHeight%22:null,%22name%22:%22medium16x9Url%22,%22rendition%22:%22medium16x9%22,%22labelOrConvertedName%22:%22medium16x9%22%7D%7D\" data-thumburl=\"/resources/media2/16x9/1024/648/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg\" data-title=\"Russia%20Ukraine%20War\" data-url=\"\" data-defaultthumb=\"true\" data-embed-file=\"%0A%20%20%20%20%3Cdiv%20class=%22sda-ImageEmbed%22%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%3Cimg%20src=%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/648/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22%20alt=%22%22%20/%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%3Cdiv%20class=%22sda-ImageEmbed-caption%22%3EIn%20this%20photo%20provided%20by%20the%20Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Services%20on%20Nov.%2021,%202024,%20rescue%20workers%20put%20out%20a%20fire%20of%20a%20building%20which%20was%20heavily%20damaged%20by%20a%20Russian%20strike%20on%20Dnipro,%20Ukraine.%20(Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Service%20via%20AP)%3C/div%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%3C/div%3E\" data-embed-type=\"image\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}The White House has batted away accusations from the Kremlin that its decision to allow the use of U.S. missiles to strike inside Russia escalated the war.{/p}{p}“The escalation, at every turn, at every step, is coming from Russia,” White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre said on Thursday.{/p}{p}While the launch of the experimental missile has put Ukraine and Russia’s NATO-allied neighbors on alert, analysts have also questioned whether Putin would follow through on threats to strike targets outside of Ukraine.{/p}{p}“Those kinds of really provocative statements are more designed for internal audiences in Russia than they are for NATO countries,” Ali said. “That's a red line that Putin probably doesn't want to cross right now and in the aftermath of President Trump's election too, I don't think he wants to be overly escalatory.”{/p}{p}President-elect Donald Trump has said he would end the war that is nearing its third anniversary on his first day in office, though he has not articulated a plan on how he will do so. Trump has pushed getting Ukraine and Russia{a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/donald-trump-pushes-ukraine-russia-toward-peace-talks-as-president-joe-biden-rushes-aid-out-military-aid-kremlin-vladimir-putin-cease-fire-volodymyr-zelenskyy\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/donald-trump-pushes-ukraine-russia-toward-peace-talks-as-president-joe-biden-rushes-aid-out-military-aid-kremlin-vladimir-putin-cease-fire-volodymyr-zelenskyy\"} to agree to end the fighting {/a}and has also reportedly warned Putin against further escalating the conflict.{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-embed-file=\"%3Cdiv%3E%3Cscript%3Ewindow.tagboardDomain%20=%20%22https://embed.tagboard.com%22;%3C/script%3E%3Cdiv%20class=%22tagboard-embed%22%20tgb-embed-id=%2214253%22%3E%3C/div%3E%3Cscript%20src=%22https://static.tagboard.com/embed/assets/js/embed.js%22%3E%3C/script%3E%3C/div%3E\" data-embed-type=\"code\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}Throughout the war, Putin has frequently sought to test the U.S. and West on its red lines in the war, targeting civilian areas and infrastructure, bringing North Korean troops into the fold and other steps as he has tried to outlast the will for continued support for Ukraine.{/p}{p}The future of U.S. support for Ukraine is unclear, as Trump has been skeptical of spending more taxpayer dollars on the war or continuing to send military aid from U.S. stockpiles that will need to be replenished.{/p}{p}Putin also signed a revised nuclear doctrine that would allow a nuclear response to an attack on Russia by a nation that is supported by a nuclear power, another reference to the supplying of military aid to Ukraine from the U.S. and others. Russian officials have said it is capable of carrying nuclear devices, though that it was without one is another indication that the Kremlin is unlikely to take that step.{/p}{p}Ukrainian defense official Kyrylo Budanov noted the lack of nuclear warheads was a signal of restraint, according to a {a href=\"https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/1029392.html\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/1029392.html\"}Ukrainian news agency.{/a}{/p}{p}“The fact that they used it in a nuclear-free version is, as they say, a warning from them, that they are not completely crazy,\" Budanov said.{/p}{p}FRIDAY: Mostly sunny, with highs in the mid 50s. Winds are light and out of the northeast.{/p}{p}SATURDAY: Partly cloudy and warmer. Highs will be in the mid 60s, with a southeasterly wind at 10-15 mph.{/p}{p}Gusty winds return Sunday, boosting temperatures to the low to mid 70s. Another dry cold front arrives Monday, dropping our highs to the mid 50s. We're still watching a system that is set to arrive by the middle of the holiday week. Rain chances will return to the forecast sometime near and around Thanksgiving, but nothing terribly impactful looks likely at this time. We will keep watching.{/p}{p}Available {a href=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\"}HERE{/a} for iOS Devices from the Apple Store{br}{/p}{p}Available {a href=\"https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.ktul.android.weather&pcampaignid=web_share\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.ktul.android.weather&pcampaignid=web_share\"}HERE{/a} for Android Devices from the Google Play Store{/p}{p}You can also follow the News title=\"https://www.facebook.com/KevinCoskrenWRDW\"}FACEBOOK{/a}{br}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/KevinCoskrenTV\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/KevinCoskrenTV\"}X (formerly known as Twitter){/a}{/p}{p}{strong}Meteorologist Joie Bettenhausen:{/strong}{br}{a href=\"https://www.facebook.com/MeteorologistJoieBettenhausen\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://www.facebook.com/MeteorologistJoieBettenhausen\"}FACEBOOK{/a}{br}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/JoieBWeather\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/JoieBWeather\"}X (formerly Twitter){/a}{/p}{p}{strong}Meteorologist Jack Gerfen:{/strong}{br}{a href=\"https://www.facebook.com/MeteorologistJackGerfen\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://www.facebook.com/MeteorologistJackGerfen\"}FACEBOOK{/a}{br}{a href=\"https://twitter.com/JackGerfenWX\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://twitter.com/JackGerfenWX\"}X (Formerly known as Twitter){/a}{/p}{p}{strong}Meteorologist Elliot Wilson:{/strong}{br}{a href=\"https://www.facebook.com/Meteorologistelliotwilson\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://www.facebook.com/Meteorologistelliotwilson\"}FACEBOOK{/a}{/p}{p}{br}{/p}{p}Russia is ramping up its threats to Western nations that have backed Ukraine’s defense of its invasion with the launch of a nuclear-capable missile and {a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/top-videos/putin-signs-russia-revised-nuclear-doctrine-after-us-president-joe-biden-support-for-ukraines-missile-strikes-western-supplied-longer-range-missiles-nato-zelenskyy-kyiv#\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/top-videos/putin-signs-russia-revised-nuclear-doctrine-after-us-president-joe-biden-support-for-ukraines-missile-strikes-western-supplied-longer-range-missiles-nato-zelenskyy-kyiv#\"}changes to its nuclear doctrine {/a}in response to the approval of American and British weapons being approved to strike deeper into Russia.{/p}{p}The launch of an experimental missile, named Oreshnik, into Ukraine on Thursday marked another escalation in the conflict that has dragged on for nearly three years. Russian President Vladimir Putin said the missile flies at ten times the speed of sound and could be turned on any nation whose missiles are used to attack Russia.{/p}{p}The test of the missile comes just days after President Joe Biden reversed a long-standing decision to allow Ukraine to use U.S.-provided missiles to strike inside Russian territory. The delivery of the Army Tactical Missile System, ballistic missiles with a range of 190 miles, is not thought to be sufficient to change the outcome of the war but it seems to have sparked new concerns inside the Kremlin that more powerful missiles in the future.{/p}{p}“This is a ground war that's a stalemate, and it's a war of attrition, and now it's the battle of wills,” said Javed Ali, a former counterterrorism official in federal government and associate professor of practice at the University of Michigan. “Even with that capability that Ukraine has now, it can't hit targets deeper in Russia, and the further they try to move them to the border, the more they also become a target. The Russians could hit those things on their side, just from across the border as well. This is the game of cat-and-mouse that's going to go on. But I don't think they qualitatively change the nature of the fighting.”{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-embed-file=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/CkKZqB1RA88?si=odPFLN9CxyLhO4yo\" data-embed-type=\"youtube\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}After the first missile was launched into Russia, the Kremlin has ramped up its rhetoric and escalated its moves in a war that has mostly been a stalemate with little gains in either direction. Putin and other top Kremlin officials have issued direct warnings to Western allies of Ukraine about providing military aid to defend its invasion, including threats that its new missile {a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/nato-and-ukraine-to-hold-emergency-talks-after-russian-hypersonic-missile-strike-putin-kremlin-oreshnik-missile-retaliation-kyiv-western-longer-range-missiles-north-korean-troops-kim-jong-un-zelenskyy#\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/nato-and-ukraine-to-hold-emergency-talks-after-russian-hypersonic-missile-strike-putin-kremlin-oreshnik-missile-retaliation-kyiv-western-longer-range-missiles-north-korean-troops-kim-jong-un-zelenskyy#\"}could be directed at NATO-allied countries.{/a}{/p}{p}“We believe that we have the right to use our weapons against military facilities of the countries that allow to use their weapons against our facilities,” Putin said in a televised speech to Russians.{/p}{p}President Joe Biden had resisted Ukrainian entreaties to approve the use of long-range missiles striking inside Ukraine for months over concerns about Russian escalation. The administration reversed that decision after North Korean troops were brought to help Russia in a new escalation of the war.{/p}{p}An initial hesitation to ramp up the type of military aid or capabilities to Ukraine before ultimately relenting has been a somewhat consistent theme throughout the war from the U.S. The White House has reversed initial rejections of requests for advanced tanks and fighter jets only to reverse course months later.{/p}{p}“It should not cause any of the friends of Ukraine to change course, that's the important thing,” said Ian Kelly, ambassador in residence at Northwestern University and a former State Department diplomat. “He's had some success in getting the Biden administration and some central European countries to kind of self-deter, and not give the Ukrainians really what they need in terms of air support and then artillery support for fear of triggering Putin.”{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-alt=\"AP24326521776338.jpg\" data-thumburl=\"/resources/media2/16x9/1024/648/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg\" data-title=\"Russia%20Ukraine%20War\" data-url=\"\" data-defaultthumb=\"true\" data-embed-file=\"%0A%20%20%20%20%3Cdiv%20class=%22sda-ImageEmbed%22%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%3Cimg%20src=%22/resources/media2/16x9/1024/648/0x53/90/7a1c48b7-2754-4885-a504-fbdc34c8676e-AP24326521776338.jpg%22%20alt=%22%22%20/%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%3Cdiv%20class=%22sda-ImageEmbed-caption%22%3EIn%20this%20photo%20provided%20by%20the%20Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Services%20on%20Nov.%2021,%202024,%20rescue%20workers%20put%20out%20a%20fire%20of%20a%20building%20which%20was%20heavily%20damaged%20by%20a%20Russian%20strike%20on%20Dnipro,%20Ukraine.%20(Ukrainian%20Emergency%20Service%20via%20AP)%3C/div%3E%0A%20%20%20%20%3C/div%3E\" data-embed-type=\"image\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}The White House has batted away accusations from the Kremlin that its decision to allow the use of U.S. missiles to strike inside Russia escalated the war.{/p}{p}“The escalation, at every turn, at every step, is coming from Russia,” White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre said on Thursday.{/p}{p}While the launch of the experimental missile has put Ukraine and Russia’s NATO-allied neighbors on alert, analysts have also questioned whether Putin would follow through on threats to strike targets outside of Ukraine.{/p}{p}“Those kinds of really provocative statements are more designed for internal audiences in Russia than they are for NATO countries,” Ali said. “That's a red line that Putin probably doesn't want to cross right now and in the aftermath of President Trump's election too, I don't think he wants to be overly escalatory.”{/p}{p}President-elect Donald Trump has said he would end the war that is nearing its third anniversary on his first day in office, though he has not articulated a plan on how he will do so. Trump has pushed getting Ukraine and Russia{a href=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/donald-trump-pushes-ukraine-russia-toward-peace-talks-as-president-joe-biden-rushes-aid-out-military-aid-kremlin-vladimir-putin-cease-fire-volodymyr-zelenskyy\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://thenationaldesk.com/news/americas-news-now/donald-trump-pushes-ukraine-russia-toward-peace-talks-as-president-joe-biden-rushes-aid-out-military-aid-kremlin-vladimir-putin-cease-fire-volodymyr-zelenskyy\"} to agree to end the fighting {/a}and has also reportedly warned Putin against further escalating the conflict.{/p}{p}{sd-embed class=\"sd-embedded-media\" data-embed-file=\"%3Cdiv%3E%3Cscript%3Ewindow.tagboardDomain%20=%20%22https://embed.tagboard.com%22;%3C/script%3E%3Cdiv%20class=%22tagboard-embed%22%20tgb-embed-id=%2214253%22%3E%3C/div%3E%3Cscript%20src=%22https://static.tagboard.com/embed/assets/js/embed.js%22%3E%3C/script%3E%3C/div%3E\" data-embed-type=\"code\" contenteditable=\"false\"}{/sd-embed}{/p}{p}Throughout the war, Putin has frequently sought to test the U.S. and West on its red lines in the war, targeting civilian areas and infrastructure, bringing North Korean troops into the fold and other steps as he has tried to outlast the will for continued support for Ukraine.{/p}{p}The future of U.S. support for Ukraine is unclear, as Trump has been skeptical of spending more taxpayer dollars on the war or continuing to send military aid from U.S. stockpiles that will need to be replenished.{/p}{p}Putin also signed a revised nuclear doctrine that would allow a nuclear response to an attack on Russia by a nation that is supported by a nuclear power, another reference to the supplying of military aid to Ukraine from the U.S. and others. Russian officials have said it is capable of carrying nuclear devices, though that it was without one is another indication that the Kremlin is unlikely to take that step.{/p}{p}Ukrainian defense official Kyrylo Budanov noted the lack of nuclear warheads was a signal of restraint, according to a {a href=\"https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/1029392.html\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://en.interfax.com.ua/news/general/1029392.html\"}Ukrainian news agency.{/a}{/p}{p}“The fact that they used it in a nuclear-free version is, as they say, a warning from them, that they are not completely crazy,\" Budanov said.{/p}{p}FRIDAY: Mostly sunny, with highs in the mid 50s. Winds are light and out of the northeast.{/p}{p}SATURDAY: Partly cloudy and warmer. Highs will be in the mid 60s, with a southeasterly wind at 10-15 mph.{/p}{p}Gusty winds return Sunday, boosting temperatures to the low to mid 70s. Another dry cold front arrives Monday, dropping our highs to the mid 50s. We're still watching a system that is set to arrive by the middle of the holiday week. Rain chances will return to the forecast sometime near and around Thanksgiving, but nothing terribly impactful looks likely at this time. We will keep watching.{/p}{p}Available {a href=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\" target=\"_blank\" title=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ktul-wx/id489182496\"}HERE{/a} for iOS
